1000多名家长签署公开信，呼吁禁止在学校进行面部识别。
随着孩子们返回学校，家长们对侵入性监控技术在面对面和远程学习中被使用发出了警报，并呼吁立法者和学校管理人员禁止面部识别。
在不到一周的时间里，来自所有50个州的1000多名家长已经签署了一封来自家长的公开信，反对在学校使用面部识别。
这封写给立法者和学校管理人员的信呼吁为了孩子的安全而禁止这项技术，并特别强调了持续监控的未知心理危险，如果有人获得面部识别系统收集的数据，可能会带来的身体危险，以及这将如何加剧对有色人种学生、女孩和性别不一致儿童的歧视。
这封信是BanFacialRecognition.com背后的数字权利组织Fight for the Future新努力的一部分，该组织将专门针对K-12学校。永久禁止面部识别的势头正在增强，但我们知道，公司专门针对学校，这就是为什么我们现在需要禁止它，凯特琳·西利·乔治(她/她)说，她(她)是两个年幼孩子的家长。这些科技公司更关心的是赚钱，而不是他们的产品会给孩子带来多大伤害。我们已经看到面部识别如何危及成年人，我们不能让这种事情发生在我们的孩子身上。
今年夏天早些时候，纽约州成为第一个通过暂停学校面部识别的州(该法案目前正在等待库默州长的签署)。与此同时，堪萨斯州的一所学校宣布，它正在使用面部识别作为新冠肺炎大流行期间重新开学计划的一部分。
除了在信上签名外，一些家长还添加了他们的个人评论，分享为什么他们不希望这项技术被用于自己的孩子。
泰娜是一位来自纽约的家长，她写道：在美国抚养一个黑人儿子意味着要时刻警惕和担心我孩子的安全。像面部识别这样的种族主义监控软件会让我的孩子在学校变得更不安全，而不是更安全。这项技术只会放大现有的歧视，并满足学校到监狱的管道。我很高兴纽约已经禁止学校对我们的孩子使用生物识别监控。我希望这个国家的其他人也能效仿，因为如果没有贪婪的科技公司在我们孩子的学校里进行不道德的实验，家长们就有足够的担忧了。
来自加利福尼亚州的一位家长苏珊娜说，我反对我们的孩子进行面部识别。建立一个关于儿童的数据库有利于获得权力和对人口的控制。现在是时候将这些策略从我们的社会中移除了。平等、安宁和减少等级控制系统是我们社会的发展方向，所以上车，朝着人民关心人民而不是人民控制人民的新范式前进。&#34；
在马萨诸塞州，萨沙写道，孩子们不能有意义地同意他们参与这类技术--随着技术的发展，我们甚至需要相当大的保障措施，才能让成年人自己给予知情同意！所有行业/政府层面对这项技术的使用都需要暂停，直到真正的道德基准到位并能够得到维护。
来自佛罗里达的亚瑟写道，这是一种控制和惩罚儿童的离谱企图，是对隐私的重大明目张胆的侵犯。面部识别在学校里是没有立足之地的。
自去年以来，为未来而战一直在领导一场得到其他数十个基层组织支持的全国性运动，呼吁彻底禁止执法和政府使用面部识别。今年2月，该组织扩大了努力，明确呼吁立法者也禁止私人个人、机构和公司在公共场所、出于监控目的或在未经受试者知情和肯定同意的情况下使用这项技术，例如解锁手机。该组织在春季发起了一项努力，将面部识别排除在大学校园之外，并让60多所学校承诺不使用这项技术。该组织还为当地推动禁令的活动人士提供支持，包括俄勒冈州波特兰最近禁止政府和私人使用面部识别。
title: 1000多名家长签署公开信，呼吁禁止在学校进行面部识别
title_en: More than 1,000 Parents Sign Open Letter Calling for a Ban on Facial Recognition in Schools

苹果在新的法律文件中回应了Etic要求这家苹果手机制造商将堡垒之夜恢复到应用商店的要求，辩称该公司的伤害“完全是自己造成的”，堡垒之夜可以随时返回iOS-只要Epic一开始删除引发游戏下架的自定义应用内支付系统即可。
苹果在长达37页的反对党简报中写道：“EPIC纵火，并在上面浇了汽油，现在请求本法院紧急协助扑灭它，”苹果在长达37页的反对党简报中写道。“[E]尽管Epicc可以通过简单地遵守多年来一直有利可图地支配其与苹果关系的合同条款，在一瞬间做到这一点。”
后来，该公司补充道：“Etic本可以避免涉及堡垒之夜和虚幻引擎的任何进一步伤害-只需简单的按键。”它说，通过拒绝这样做，Etic正在“劫持自己的客户作为人质，以获得在商业纠纷中的筹码。”
这并不是苹果公司的新论点。自从8月份将堡垒之夜踢出应用商店以来，这家苹果手机制造商一直明确表示，它很乐意回到现状。但这些文件确实提供了更多细节，说明苹果在回应Etic要求初步禁令和恢复堡垒之夜对iOS用户的访问时将利用的法律论据。决定此事的全面法庭听证会定于9月28日举行。
在整个申请过程中，苹果重申了其主要论点：该公司通过维护App Store提供了一项有价值的服务，通过规避商店的规定，Etic违反了与苹果的合同，该公司可以自由地将其从其服务中删除。但反对派的简报也增加了新的细节。例如，苹果表示，Etic发起这场法律战在一定程度上是为了让人们注意到一个萎靡不振的特许经营权：
“由于与史诗公司对苹果的指控无关的原因，堡垒之夜的人气正在下降。截至2020年7月，对堡垒之夜的兴趣较2019年10月减少近七成。这起诉讼(以及它引发的头条新闻)似乎是旨在重振人们对堡垒之夜兴趣的营销活动的一部分。
此外，苹果指出，iOS在堡垒之夜的营收中并不占很大比例。它引用了EpicePic的披露，只有10%的堡垒之夜消费者经常玩苹果手机，并声称EpicePic说苹果公司在收入方面是“最小的一块蛋糕”。同样，言下之意是，Etic并没有遭受“不可弥补的伤害”(就像该公司在自己提交的文件中声称的那样)，而是出于其他利己主义的原因而掀起了轩然大波。
与这一论点相平行的是，苹果公司表示，Etic声称它因被App Store下架而遭受“声誉损害”的说法也是误导性的。这家iPhone制造商表示，Etic的“事先计划好的媒体闪电战”表明，它实际上是在欢迎由这起案件引起的关注：
“如果Etic真的担心它会因为这场争端而名誉受损，它就不会参与这些精心策划的宣传努力。从所有迹象来看(包括#免费论坛活动)，Etic认为自己在这里的行为会产生善意，提升声誉，并将用户吸引到堡垒之夜那里，而不是相反。这不是坏事。“。
不过，这只是苹果更广泛的论点中的一小部分。如果你想为本月的法庭听证会做准备，你可以阅读下面的反对派简报全文：
note_en:
Apple has responded to Epic’s demands that the iPhone-maker  restore  Fortnite to the App Store in new legal filings, arguing that the company’s injuries are “entirely self-inflicted” and that  Fortnite can return to iOS at any time — just as soon as Epic removes the custom in-app payment system that triggered the game’s removal in the first place.
 “Epic started a fire, and poured gasoline on it, and now asks this Court for emergency assistance in putting it out,” writes Apple in the 37-page  opposition brief. “[E]ven though Epic can do so itself in an instant by simply adhering to the contractual terms that have profitably governed its relationship with Apple for years.”
  Later, the company adds: “Epic could have avoided any further harm involving both  Fortnite and Unreal Engine — with a simple keystroke.” It says that by refusing to do so Epic is “holding its own customers hostage to gain leverage in a business dispute.”
 This is not a new argument from Apple. Ever since it kicked  Fortnite off  the App Store in August, the iPhone-maker has been clear it would be happy to return to the  status quo. But these filings do offer more detail on the legal arguments Apple will be drawing on in its response to Epic’s demand for a  preliminary injunction and the restoration of  Fortnite to iOS users. A full court hearing to decide that matter is scheduled for September 28th.
 Throughout the filing Apple reiterates its main argument: that the company provides a valuable service by maintaining the App Store, and that by circumventing the store’s rules, Epic has broken its contract with Apple and the company is free to boot it from its services. But the opposition brief also adds new details. Apple suggests, for example, that Epic has started this legal battle in part to draw attention to a flagging franchise:
 “For reasons having nothing to do with Epic’s claims against Apple, Fortnite’s popularity is on the wane. By July 2020, interest in Fortnite had decreased by nearly 70% as compared to October 2019. This lawsuit (and the front-page headlines it has generated) appears to be part of a marketing campaign designed to reinvigorate interest in Fortnite.”
 Elsewhere, Apple notes that iOS is not a big part of  Fortnite’s revenue. It quotes disclosures from Epic that only 10 percent of  Fortnite consumers play regularly on the iPhone, and claims that Epic has said Apple is the “smallest piece of the pie’” when it comes to revenue. Again, the implication is that Epic is not suffering “irreparable harm” (as the company has claimed in its own  filings) but is kicking up a fuss for other, self-interested reasons.
 In parallel with this argument, Apple says Epic’s claim that it’s suffered “reputational harm” from being booted off the App Store is also misleading. The iPhone maker says Epic’s “pre-planned media blitz” shows that it is, in fact, welcoming attention created by this case:
 “If Epic were truly concerned that it would suffer reputational injury from this dispute, it would not be engaging in these elaborate efforts to publicize it. From all appearances (including the #freefortnite campaign), Epic thinks its conduct here will engender goodwill, boost its reputation, and drive users to Fortnite, not the opposite. That is not harm.”
 That’s only a small part of Apple’s wider argument, though. If you want to prepare for this month’s court hearing, you can read the opposition brief in full below:
title: 苹果在新的堡垒之夜提交的文件中指责EpicePic“纵火并向其倾倒汽油”
title_en: Apple accuses Epic of ‘starting a fire and pouring gasoline on it’ in new Fortnite filing

“这绝对是最重要的社会话题和全球问题之一，”Tee在她的中断会议上说(视频嵌入在本文的底部)。
Homage的平台四年前在新加坡成立，使用婚介引擎帮助家庭找到最好的照顾者，而其远程健康平台提供在线医疗咨询和筛查等服务。自那以后，它已在马来西亚推出，并于昨日宣布了日本最大的医疗保健技术公司之一Infocom的一项新战略投资。这一伙伴关系将使Homage能够加快其亚太扩张。
在开始致敬之前，Tee是总部位于纽约的Rockettrip的联合创始人。Rockettrip是一个为降低公司与工作旅行相关的成本而创建的机票预订平台，吸引了谷歌风险投资公司(Google Ventures)、Y Combinator和贝塞默风险投资公司(Bessemer Ventures)等投资者，并筹集了超过3000万美元。但在2016年，在国外生活了大约15年后，Tee决定回到她的祖国新加坡。在她的中断会议上，Tee说这是为了更接近她的母亲，因为她觉得她的创业经验也可以应用到东南亚。
Te知道她想成立另一家公司，但她没有决定立即解决照看空间的问题。当她的几个近亲被诊断出患有需要特殊护理的慢性病时，这个想法变成了现实。
“我们不知道如何应对，甚至不知道如何开始思考需要什么，就在那时，我意识到，哇，我需要在很多方面训练自己，”Tee说，“我们不知道如何应对，甚至不知道如何开始考虑需要什么，那时候我意识到，哇，我需要在很多方面训练自己。
随着人口老龄化和社会动态的变化，世界各地的许多家庭都在应对同样的挑战。传统上本应照看亲属的家庭成员无法这样做，因为他们已经搬走或需要工作。
家庭通常依靠口碑或机构来寻找照顾者，这是一个复杂、耗时且往往在情感上困难的过程。Homage使用匹配算法使其更容易。这个平台最独特的地方之一就是它有多少细节。提供者不仅根据他们的证书和他们提供的护理类型(例如，长期护理、暂缓护理、物理治疗或康复)进行筛选，而且还根据特定的技能进行筛选。例如，许多患者需要行动辅助，因此Homage会评估他们能够安全地进行哪种转移。
然后，它的匹配技术决定哪些护理人员最适合病人，最后的任务由Homage的工作人员进行。通过简化流程，Homage还降低了成本，使更多的人能够获得其服务，同时提高了提供商的费率。
更高的薪酬利用了Homage的另一个目标：扩大市场中的护理人员池，留住人才。它解决这个问题的其他方式是将其平台上的护理员安排到他们最适合的工作中，组织继续教育计划，并确保他们的日程安排不会超额。平台上的一些护理员有长期合同，而另一些人一周只有几天与致敬客户一起工作。
今年6月，Homage推出了远程医疗服务。这个名为致敬健康的平台已经开发了一段时间，但由于新冠肺炎的大流行，它的推出速度加快了。远程会诊符合该公司业务的“高接触”或面对面护理，因为许多患者需要定期筛查或咨询医生和专家。对于行动不便或免疫功能低下的患者，这使得他们更容易进行常规会诊。
Tee说，包括可穿戴传感器在内的硬件也显示出在需要急性护理之前识别潜在健康问题的希望，比如心脏问题，但其中一个挑战是让患者更容易融入日常生活或记得佩戴。
总体而言，Homage的使命是创建一个涵盖多种护理需求的整体平台。它与战略投资者Infocom的新合作伙伴关系将有助于推进这一目标，因为该公司与日本约1.3万家设施合作，包括老年住宅和医院。Tee表示，Homage已经与该公司洽谈了几年。Infocom为广泛的垂直领域开发软件，包括药品、医院和医疗记录管理以及医学成像。
Infocom还运营着自己的护理平台，它与Homage的合作将使两家公司能够合作，接触到更多的患者。日本是世界上老年人口最多的国家之一。TEE说，为了满足需求，日本在未来5到10年内至少需要动员50万名照顾者。
“我们需要开始建设基础设施，使人们能够获得他们需要的那种护理服务，所以我们在这一使命上真正与Infocom保持一致，”Tee说。“他们也有一个让照顾者申请日本工作的平台，他们认为致敬模式特别适用，因为它也是经过精心策划的。”
note_en:
“It’s absolutely one of the most important social topics and global issues,” Tee said during her Disrupt session (the video is embedded at the bottom of this article).
 Founded in Singapore four years ago,  Homage’s   platform uses a matchmaking engine to help families find the best caregivers, while its telehealth platform provides services like online medical consultations and screenings. It has since launched in Malaysia and yesterday  announced a new strategic investment from Infocom, one of the largest healthcare technology companies in Japan. The partnership will enable Homage to accelerate its Asia-Pacific expansion.
  Before starting Homage, Tee was co-founder of New York-based Rocketrip. A ticket-booking platform created to reduce work travel-related costs for companies, Rocketrip attracted investors like Google Ventures, Y Combinator and Bessemer Ventures, and raised more than $30 million. But in 2016, Tee decided to return to Singapore, her home country, after living abroad for about 15 years. In her Disrupt session, Tee said this was to be closer to her mother, and because she felt that her startup experience could also be applied to Southeast Asia.
 Tee knew that she wanted to launch another company, but she didn’t decide to tackle the caregiving space immediately. That idea materialized when several of her close relatives were diagnosed with chronic conditions that needed specialized care.
 “We didn’t know how to cope or how even to start thinking about what was required, and that was when I realized, wow, I needed to get myself schooled in many ways,” Tee said.
 Many families around the world are dealing with the same challenges as their populations age and social dynamics shift. Family members who traditionally would have been carers for relatives are unable to do so because they have moved away or need to work.
 Families often rely on word-of-mouth or agencies to find caregivers, a complicated, time-intensive and often emotionally difficult process. Homage uses matching algorithms to make it easier. One of the most unique things about the platform is how much detail it goes into. Providers are not only screened based on their certifications and the kind of care they provide (for example, long-term care, respite care, physical therapy or rehabilitation), but specific skills. For example, many patients need mobility assistance, so Homage assesses what kind of transfers they are able to safely perform.
 Then its matching technology decides which caregivers are best suited for a patient, and final assignments are made by Homage’s staff. By streamlining the process, Homage also lowers its costs, making its services accessible to more people while increasing pay rates for providers.
 Better pay taps into another one of Homage’s goals: expanding the caregiving pool in its markets and retaining talent. Other ways it addresses the issue is by placing caregivers on its platform into the jobs they are best suited for, organizing continuing education programs and making sure they are not over-scheduled. Some caregivers on the platform have long-term contracts, while others work with Homage clients only a few days a week.
  In June, Homage  launched its telehealth service. Called Homage Health, the platform has been in development for a while, but its launch was accelerated because of the COVID-19 pandemic. Remote consultations fit into the “high-touch,” or in-person, care side of the company’s business because many patients need regular screenings or consultations with doctors and specialists. For patients who have limited mobility or are immunocompromised, this makes it easier for them to make routine consults.
 Hardware, including wearable sensors, also show promise to identify potential health issues, like heart conditions, before they require acute care, but one challenge is making them easier for patients to integrate into their daily routines or remember to wear, Tee said.
 Overall, Homage’s mission is to create a holistic platform that covers many caregiving needs. Its new partnership with strategic investor Infocom will help bring that forward because the company, which Tee said Homage has been talking to for several years, works with about 13,000 facilities in Japan, including senior residences and hospitals. Infocom develops software for a wide range of verticals, including drug, hospital and medical record management, and medical imaging.
 Infocom also runs its own caregiving platform, and its partnership with Homage will enable the two companies to collaborate and reach more patients. Japan has one of the largest populations of elderly people in the world. Tee said at minimum, half a million caregivers need to be mobilized within the next five to 10 years in Japan in order to meet demand.
 “We need to start building infrastructure to enable people to be able to access the kind of care services that they need, and so we really align in terms of that mission with Infocom,” said Tee. “They also have a platform that engages caregivers to apply for jobs in Japan and they see the Homage model as being particularly applicable because it’s curated as well.”
title: Homage的Gillian Tee谈技术如何为世界老龄化人口服务
title_en: Homage’s Gillian Tee on how technology can serve the world’s aging population

这位智库主席表示，这使中国成为用户规模最大的5G市场。这一里程碑发生在中国各大运营商为消费者推出5G计划不到一年后，也是在当局开始发放5G商用牌照一年多一点之后。
这个数字仍然只占总订阅量的一小部分。6月份，中国三大国有运营商合计拥有约16亿移动用户(表明中国14亿人口人均拥有超过一台移动设备)。
中国的5G雄心是政府、网络运营商、电信设备制造商、设备制造商和软件开发商多管齐下的努力。政策制定者需要向消费者展示网络速度的明显改善，因此运营商一直在全国范围内积极建立5G基站-截至7月，5G基站超过46万座。
一位官员在7月份表示，中国平均每周新增1.5万个5G基站。政府计划到2020年底将这一数字提高到60万人，覆盖全国所有地级城市。中国5G努力的一个明显赢家是华为，该公司生产5G设备和支撑下一代网络的基础设施。
与此同时，华为、OPPO、小米及其竞争对手都在争先恐后地推出5G兼容手机。根据CAICT最近发布的数据，今年到目前为止，中国已经售出了超过9300万部5G手机。8月份，5G手机占总出货量的60%。
中国向5G的快速转变也推动了对集成电路等新硬件部件的需求。一位行业官员在7月份表示，2020年上半年，中国IC产量超过1000亿台，同比增长16.4%，并补充说，大部分需求来自5G相关项目。
note_en:
That makes China the largest 5G market in terms of user size, the think tank president said. The milestone came in less than a year after China’s top carriers  rolled out  5G plans for consumers, and just over a year after the authority began issuing 5G licenses  for commercial use.
 The number is still a small fraction of the overall subscription. In June, China’s three state-run carriers collectively commanded  some 1.6 billion mobile subscribers (suggesting China’s 1.4 billion population owned over one mobile device per capita).
  China’s 5G ambition is a multi-pronged effort among the government, network carriers, telecoms equipment makers, device makers, and software developers. Policymakers need to show consumers visible improvement on network speed, and as such the carriers have been aggressively setting up 5G base stations across the country — more than 460,000 towers by July.
 China was adding an average of 15,000 new 5G base stations every week,  said an official in July. The government has plans to raise that number to 600,000 by the end of 2020, covering all prefectural-level cities nationwide. A clear winner in China’s 5G push is Huawei, which makes both 5G devices and the infrastructure that undergirds the next-gen network.
 In the meantime, Huawei, Oppo, Xiaomi and their rivals are rushing to launch 5G compatible handsets. China has sold over 93 million units of 5G mobile phones this year so far, according to  recent data released by CAICT. 5G phones accounted for 60% of total shipments in August.
 China’s rapid shift to 5G is also driving the need for new hardware parts like integrated circuits. The country produced over 100 billion units of ICs during the first half of 2020, representing a 16.4% year-over-year gain,  said an industry official in July, adding that much of the demand came from 5G-related projects.
title: 中国在不到一年的时间里突破了1.1亿5G用户
title_en: China tops 110 million 5G users in less than a year

note:
种子轮由Kindred Capital牵头，Point Nine Capital和之前的投资者Forward Partners参与其中。这使得重力草图筹集的总金额达到540万美元。此外，这家初创公司之前还获得了InnovationRCA和詹姆斯·戴森基金会(James Dyson Foundation)的赠款资金。
重力草图由奥卢瓦西伊·索萨尼亚(Oluwaeyi Sosanya)、丹妮拉·帕雷德斯(Daniela Paredes)和丹尼尔·托马斯(Daniel Thomas)于2014年创立，希望改变实体产品的设计、开发和推向市场的方式。它为跨学科团队提供3D设计软件，以便他们能够以一种更加顺畅的方式“创建、协作和审查”，包括通过虚拟现实进行3D和实时协作。这个想法是为了帮助加快开发周期，特别是让分布在全球各地、越来越偏远的团队参与进来。
重力草图首席执行官兼联合创始人奥卢瓦西伊·索萨尼亚(Oluwaeyi Sosanya)表示：“随着时间范围的缩短，消费者对产品的要求更快、功能更多、生产更可持续，协作变得越来越重要。”跨国公司在全球各地的设计和工程团队也在激增，他们需要保持联系，才能像以前在同一地点一样准确地交付产品。为大公司服务的中小型设计公司也必须采用这种方法才能赢得业务-他们经常从国际客户那里获得工作，这些客户无法像国内客户那样频繁地面对面会面，而且还被要求达到极高的交付标准“。
除了更快的产品周期和远程工作带来的压力外，产品设计过程本身并不总是最优的，涉及到拥有不同学科和软件工具的多个团队，并从2D跃升到3D。“当我们谈到设计实体产品时，我们想象的是3D形式的物体，”Sosanya说。“然而，多年来，我们不得不通过2D媒介或粗略的物理模型来提出这个想法。所有的实体产品都是从2D草图开始，然后费心地转换成数字3D模型，然后通过标准的制造工艺来生产。“。
为了缓解这一问题，重力草图将设计师从最初的草图阶段带入数字3D空间，这让他们能够更好地控制最初的想法以及它是如何发展的。然后，完整的设计团队可以加入同一个VR空间，在投入时间和资源之前，从设计师的角度全面了解设计。
“设计师可以在构思阶段更准确地让所有利益相关者达成一致，”索萨尼亚解释说。有了虚拟现实，我们可以利用每个人都以3D思考的事实，并提供一种绕过每个设计过程中都存在的2D可视化步骤的解决方案，这样用户就可以以3D思考并以3D进行创作。这有点像3D的Zoom会议，帮助每个人从自己的角度理解尚未实现的产品“。
此外，在重力草图中创建的内容可以进一步推进设计流程，这意味着不需要创建设计的不同视图，也不需要在其他工具中创建三维模型。重力草图设计可以输出到市场上几乎所有的CAD工具，声称准确度为100%。
这似乎也引起了一些世界领先公司的共鸣，如福特、日产和锐步，它们使用的是重力草图，以及全球60所大学和超过5万名创意专业人士。
与此同时，GravitySketch表示，新的资金将使公司能够扩大平台规模，使其“完全不依赖于硬件”。它目前可以与一系列虚拟现实硬件配合使用，目前还在iPad、移动设备和台式机的测试版中。
note_en:
The seed round was led by Kindred Capital, with participation from Point Nine Capital and previous investor Forward Partners. It brings the total amount raised by Gravity Sketch to $5.4 million. In addition, the startup previously received grant funding from InnovationRCA and the James Dyson Foundation.
 Founded in 2014 by Oluwaseyi Sosanya, Daniela Paredes and Daniel Thomas, Gravity Sketch wants to change the way physical products are designed, developed, and brought to market. It offers 3D design software for cross-disciplinary teams so that they can “create, collaborate, and review” in a much more frictionless way, including via virtual reality in which collaboration can take place in 3D and real-time. The idea is to help speed up development cycles, especially involving globally-distributed and increasingly remote teams.
  “Collaboration is increasingly important as time frames are shortened and consumers request products sooner, with more features, and produced more sustainably,” says Oluwaseyi Sosanya, CEO and co-founder of Gravity Sketch. “There is also a surge in multinational companies growing globally distributed design and engineering teams, who need to stay connected in order to deliver with the same accuracy they once did being in the same location. Small-to-mid-sized design firms who service large companies must also adopt this approach in order to win business — they often gain work from international clients that are unable to meet face to face as frequently as their domestic clients, and are also held to extremely high standards of delivery”.
 In addition to pressure brought about by faster product cycles and remote working, the product design process itself isn’t always optimum, involving multiple teams with different disciplines and software tools and a jump from 2D to 3D. “When we talk about designing a physical product, we’re imagining this object in 3D,” says Sosanya. “However, for many years we have had to bring out that idea through 2D mediums, or through rough physical models. All physical products start with 2D sketches, which are then painstakingly translated to digital 3D models and then produced through standard manufacturing processes”.
 To mitigate this, Gravity Sketch brings the designer into the digital 3D space from the initial sketch phase, which gives them greater control over the initial idea and how it develops. The full design team can then join the same VR space to get a full understanding of the design from the designer’s perspective before investing time and resources.
 “The designer can more accurately get all stakeholders on the same page at the ideation phase,” Sosanya explains. “With VR we can leverage the fact that everyone thinks in 3D and offer a solution that sidesteps the 2D visualisation step which is present in every design process, so users can think in 3D and create in 3D. It’s sort of like a Zoom meeting in 3D, helping everyone understand the yet to be materialised product from their own vantage point”.
  Furthermore, content created in Gravity Sketch can further the design pipeline, meaning there is no need to create different views of a design or have to create a 3D model in another tool. Gravity Sketch designs can be exported to almost all of the CAD tools on the market with a claimed 100% accuracy.
 It seems to be resonating, too, with some of the world’s leading companies, such as Ford, Nissan, and Reebok, using Gravity Sketch, alongside 60 universities and over 50,000 creative professionals worldwide.
 Meanwhile, Gravity Sketch says the new funding will enable the company to scale up the platform to become “entirely hardware-agnostic”. It currently works with a range of virtual reality hardware, and is in beta for iPad, mobile, and desktop.
title: 重力草图因其基于VR的产品设计和协作平台获得370万美元的种子
title_en: Gravity Sketch scores $3.7M seed for its VR-based product design and collaboration platform

note:
Kahoot已经收购了丹麦初创公司Actimo，后者为企业提供了一个培训员工和与员工互动的平台。Kahoot表示，此次收购将以现金和股票相结合的方式进行，并致力于将这家规模较小的公司的企业总价值提高到2600万美元至3300万美元之间，出售预计将于2020年10月完成。
在一个科技市场上，这听起来可能是一个不大的金额，在这个市场上，公司目前经常看到首轮A阶段的账面估值达到数亿美元，但它也为创始人和他们的投资者呈现了一种不同的轨迹。
根据PitchBook的数据，对于Actimo来说，这实际上是一个强劲的退出，该公司筹集的资金不到50万美元。它还将Actimo置于一家一直在全球快速扩张的公司的羽翼下，发现-与在线教育和远程工作领域的其他公司一样-目前由于新冠肺炎而导致的社交疏远状态正在提振其业务。
为了让你对Kahoot的规模和增长有一个了解，该公司表示，自2013年首次推出该平台以来，除了总计约44亿用户外，目前它拥有超过10亿活跃用户。在过去的12个月里，它的平台上已经玩了大约200场游戏。今年6月，当Kahoot宣布筹集了2800万美元的资金时，它告诉我们，已经玩了1亿场游戏。
鉴于其增长和未来的机会-即使撇开冠状病毒的发展不谈，远程工作和远程学习至少作为一种长期选择将变得更加常见-该公司的估值也有所上升。由于其部分股票在挪威Merkur市场交易，该公司目前的市值为187.16亿挪威克朗，按今天的汇率计算约为20.8亿美元。这一数字在6月份为14亿美元。
卡胡特瞄准企业部门并不是什么新鲜事。多年来，该公司一直在这一领域建立业务。它说，在过去的12个月里，它记录了2000万名参与企业培训“游戏”的“玩家”的200万次会话，其中约97%的财富500强用户是这些用户。客户包括Facebook(用于销售培训)、Oyo(接待培训和入职)和Qualys(用于在会议期间进行民意调查)等。
关键的是，虽然Kahoot的很多受众都在教育行业，但它的大部分收入来自公司，这也是它热衷于通过更多服务和用户来增长这一细分市场的原因之一。
卡胡特表示，Actimo的目标是打造一套旨在帮助组织培养公司文化的产品-随着许多组织现在持续8个月，并将整个团队定期在实体办公室之外工作计算在内，这已成为优先事项。
即使在最好的情况下，让团队感觉像一个团队，让个人感觉到更多的是对雇主的事务性尊重，也不是一件简单的事情。现在，随着我们继续在身体上远离彼此工作，需要更多的工具和努力才能取得正确的平衡。
在这种背景下，Actimo的解决方案只是一个方面，但可能是一个有趣的方面：它建立了一个平台，员工可以在这个平台上跟踪他们已经或需要进行的培训，与其他同事互动，并提供反馈，雇主可以使用它来总体跟踪和鼓励员工在整个公司及其各种努力中的参与情况。它的客户包括Circle K、Hi3G和Compass Group等大约200家企业，目前的ARR为500万美元。
相比之下，Kahoot在8月份发布的第二季度财务报告中公布的ARR为2,500万美元，该季度的发票收入为960万美元，同比增长约317%。该公司还从微软和迪士尼等公司筹集了约1.1亿美元的私人资金。
由于Kahoot希望找到的不仅仅是公司IT和软件架构中的一个暂时性位置-注意力的暂时性对于任何基于游戏的东西来说都是一个风险-选择Actimo并努力将该平台与其他公司工作相结合的方法是非常有意义的。你也可以想象有一天，它可能会为教育部门创建一个类似的仪表盘。
我们很高兴欢迎Actimo团队成为快速发展的Kahoot的一部分！家人，“卡胡特说！首席执行官Eilert Hanoa在一份声明中说。此次收购将进一步扩展Kahoot！公司的企业学习产品，提供为一线部门量身定做的解决方案，并巩固公司文化，巩固各种类型和规模的公司的远程和分散团队之间的互动。这延续了我们表达的雄心壮志，即通过增加我们可以在我们的全球平台上利用的战略能力，通过并购实现增长。“。
“我们很高兴能与卡胡特联手！我们的使命是开发连接远程员工并提高员工参与度和生产力的下一级解决方案，“Actimo首席执行官Eske Gurge在一份声明中表示。“成为卡胡特的一部分！凭借我们与各行各业创新和雄心勃勃的企业合作的经验，我们可以共同为企业学习和参与设定一个新的标准。“
note_en:
Kahoot has acquired Danish startup  Actimo, which provides a platform for businesses to train and engage with employees. Kahoot said that the purchase is being made with a combination of cash and shares, and works to to a total enterprise value of between $26 million and $33 million for the smaller company, with the sale expected to be completed in October 2020.
 It may sound like a modest sum in a tech market where companies are currently and regularly seeing paper valuations in the hundreds of millions at Series A stage, but it also presents a different kind of trajectory both for founders and their investors.
  This is actually a strong exit for Actimo, which had raised less than $500,000, according to data from  PitchBook. And it puts Actimo under the wing of a company that has been scaling globally fast, finding — like others in the areas of online education and remote working — that the current state of social distancing due to Covid-19 is resulting in a boost to its business.
 To give you an idea of the scale and growth of Kahoot, the company says that currently it has over 1 billion active users, on top of some 4.4 billion users in aggregate since first launching the platform in 2013. In the last 12 months, some 200 games have been played on its platform. In June, when Kahoot announced that it had  raised $28 million in funding, it told us that 100 million games had been played.
 In light of its growth and the future opportunity — even putting aside the progression of the coronavirus, it looks like remote work and remote learning will at the least become a lot more common as a longer-term option — the company has also seen a rise in its valuation. With some of its shares traded on the  Merkur Market in Norway, the company currently has a market cap of 18.716 billion Norwegian Krone, which at today’s rates is about $2.08 billion. That figure was $1.4 billion in June.
 Kahoot’s targeting of the corporate sector is not new. The company has been building a business in this space for years. It says that in the last 12 months, it logged 2 million sessions across 20 million participating “players” of its corporate training “games”, with some 97% of the Fortune 500 among those users. Customers include the likes of Facebook (for sales training), Oyo (hospitality training and onboarding) and Qualys (for taking polls during a conference), among others.
 Critically, while a lot of Kahoot’s audience is in education, its corporate most of the revenues come in, one reason why it’s keen to grow that segment with more services and users.
 The aim with Actimo, Kahoot says, is to build out a product set aimed at helping organisations with company culture — which, with many organisations now going on eight months and counting of entire teams working regularly outside of their physical offices, has grown as a priority.
 Keeping a team feeling like a team, and an individual feeling more than a transactional regard for an employer, is not a simple thing in the best of times. Now, as we continue to work physically away from each other, it will take even more tools and efforts to get the balance right.
 In that context, Actimo’s solution is just one aspect, but potentially an interesting one: it has built a platform where employees can track the training that they have done or need to do, engage with other co-workers, and provide feedback, and employers can use it to generally track and encourage how employees are engaging across the company and its various efforts. It counts some 200 enterprises, including Circle K, Hi3G, and Compass Group, among its customers, and has current ARR of $5 million.
 For comparison, Kahoot, in its  Q2 financials published in August, reported ARR of $25 million, with invoiced revenue for the quarter at $9.6 million, growing some 317% on the same quarter a year before. The company has also raised some $110 million in private funding from the likes of Microsoft and Disney.
 As Kahoot looks to find more than just a transient place in a company’s IT and software fabric — transience of attention always being a risk with anything gaming-based — it makes a lot of sense to pick up Actimo and work on ways of coupling the platform with its other corporate work. You can also imagine a time when it might create a similar kind of dashboard for the educational sector.
 “We are excited to welcome the Actimo team to be part of the fast-growing Kahoot! family,” said Kahoot! CEO, Eilert Hanoa, in a statement. “This acquisition will further extend Kahoot!’s corporate learning offerings, by providing solutions tailored for the frontline segment, as well as to solidify company culture and engagement among remote and distributed teams in companies of all types and sizes. This continues our expressed ambition to also grow through M&A by adding strategic capabilities that we can leverage across our global platform.”
 “We are thrilled to join forces with Kahoot! in our mission to develop next-level solutions that connect remote employees and boost employee engagement and productivity,” said Eske Gunge, CEO at Actimo, in a statement. “Being part of Kahoot! and with our experience from working with innovative and ambitious enterprises across industries, we can together set a new standard for corporate learning and engagement.”
title: 用户生成的电子学习网站Kahoot以高达3300万美元收购Actimo，加倍投资于企业部门
title_en: User-generated e-learning site Kahoot acquires Actimo for up to $33M to double down on corporate sector

note:
种子前的一轮融资由Paua Ventures和ByFounders牵头，Lemonade Stand、Omnisend和各种天使投资者参与其中。后者包括Bolt联合创始人马库斯和马丁·维利格、Matterport首席营销官罗宾·丹尼尔斯、PipeDrive联合创始人Ragnar Sass和Skype长期高管Sten Tamkivi等。
Out漏斗成立于2017年，其创始人是营销老手安德鲁斯·普尔德(Andrus Purde)(曾供职于Skype和Pipedrive)、安德里斯·莱因曼(Andris Reinman)(Nodemailer和WildDuck等开源电子邮件项目的创始人)和马库斯·莱明(Markus Leming)。这家初创公司开发了一种被称为“收入营销自动化工具”的工具，旨在使销售和营销职能协同工作，以推动收入增长。
“中小企业仍在努力统一销售和营销数据，”珀德告诉我。“金钱和时间浪费在设置工作流、用数字胶带连接数据库和手动提取Reports…上。这(也)意味着每个人都错失了机会，也错失了营收目标。“。
此外，销售人员没有销售对话的背景，也不知道哪些线索已经准备好购买，领导层也不容易对活动的有效性有“全局性”的可见性。“最后但并非最不重要的一点是，我们所有人都会收到大量的‘垃圾邮件’，而不是相关的信息，”他说。
为了解决这一问题，OutFunnel的秘诀是将其与CRM(目前是PipeDrive、Cu和HubSpot CRM，还有更多)的深度集成，以及各种功能，如与CRM数据同步的自动电子邮件、报告和精确定位。这家初创公司已经赢得了400多名付费客户，北美是其最大的市场，其次是较大的欧洲国家和澳大利亚。
“我们的典型客户是中小型企业，既需要销售又需要营销，而且销售周期较长，而不是交易性的，”珀德补充道。根据我们的估计，这大约占所有中小企业的25%，例如，销售专业服务、咨询、房地产、医疗保健…的企业。这就是说，我们也有一些更知名的扩大规模的客户，比如Bolt。
note_en:
The pre-seed round was led by Paua Ventures and byFounders, with participation from Lemonade Stand, Omnisend and various angel investors. The latter includes Bolt co-founders Markus and Martin Villig, Matterport CMO Robin Daniels, Pipedrive co-founder Ragnar Sass, and long-time Skype exec Sten Tamkivi, amongst others.
 Formed in 2017, Outfunnel’s founders are marketing veteran Andrus Purde (previously of Skype and Pipedrive), Andris Reinman (creator of open-source email projects like Nodemailer and WildDuck) and Markus Leming. The startup has developed what it dubs a “revenue marketing automation tool” that is designed to enable sales and marketing functions to work together to drive revenue.
  “SMBs still struggle to unite sales and marketing data,” Purde tells me. “Money and time is wasted setting up workflows, connecting databases with digital duct tape and manually pulling reports… This [also] means that everyone misses opportunities, as well as revenue goals”.
 Furthermore, salespeople have no context for sales conversations and don’t know which leads are ready to buy, and the leadership don’t easily have “big picture” visibility into the effectiveness of campaigns. “Last but not least, all of us receive lots of ‘spam’ instead of relevant messages,” he says.
 To solve this, Outfunnel’s secret sauce sees it integrate deeply with CRMs (currently Pipedrive, Copper, and Hubspot CRM, with more to follow) coupled with various features such as automated emails in sync with CRM data, reporting, and precise targeting. The startup has already won over more than 400 paying customers, with North America being its biggest market, followed by larger European countries and Australia.
 “Our typical customer is a small to medium-sized business that needs both sales and marketing and where sales cycles are longer, not transactional,” adds Purde. “That’s roughly 25% of all SMBs according to our estimations e.g. businesses selling professional services, consulting, real estate, healthcare… That said, we have some better-known scale-ups as customers, too, such as Bolt”.
title: Out漏斗获得110万欧元的预播种子，以弥合营销和销售之间的差距
title_en: Outfunnel picks up €1.1M pre-seed to bridge the gap between marketing and sales

note:
去年，鲍德顿筹集了4亿美元的基金。但是，基金的投资方式是不是因为新冠肺炎而改变了呢？
“在很多方面，一切都没有改变，”他说。20年来，我们一直是一家专注于泛欧洲的首轮风险投资公司，…。如果说有什么不同的话，我认为新冠肺炎已经展示了技术如何帮助我们度过各种挑战，我是说所有来自家庭的工作…。这真的很奇怪，不能亲自和那些…的人(企业家)在一起。但在欧洲投资科技的整体策略，跟以前完全一样。“。
虽然事情没有那么简单。例如，Balderton向汽车租赁初创公司Virtuo投资了2000万欧元。目前，旅游业并不是一个很好的领域。
钱德拉莱克承认，“今年有些行业我们遇到了挑战。”然而，他说，随着假期的到来，他们“度过了艰难的4月和5月，但实际上他们经历了一个繁荣的8月”。
“我想说的是，总的来说，大多数(初创公司)都经营得相当不错。”他指出，欧洲各国政府已经设立了支持科技公司的资金，当然，其他科技行业也蓬勃发展。
在大流行期间，许多消费者通过Zoom和Houseparty等应用程序跳入虚拟网络，但鲍德顿对一家名为Riff的隐形模式初创公司进行了少量投资，该公司与俱乐部一样，正在以一种新的方式使用音频。他暗示，这将是一场关于音频的企业游戏。
“有趣的是，现在离它最近的可能是”不和谐“，它显然已经是一个庞大的网络，但实际上是一款针对游戏玩家…的非常垂直的应用。但我认为，在企业中有机会做一些类似的事情，就像Slake可以说是从WhatsApp或Facebook Messenger的消费者信息中获得了很多最初的线索一样。我认为我们会看到类似的情况，企业获得的东西有点基于我们在消费产品中看到的东西。“。
他说，里夫解决了“在办公室里撞见某人聊天时的饮水机时刻的经典陈词滥调，在这个新的现实中，真的很难做到这一点。”
他还说，Zoom后面还有一些有趣的子市场，“也需要解决”。鲍尔德顿投资了一家名为DemoDesk(一个基于云的屏幕共享平台)的公司，例如，该公司关注的是“网络研讨会、销售会议以及类似的特定对话，这些场合的需求略有不同。”
钱德拉莱克认为，这个世界将不得不与新冠肺炎共处多年，但新的解决方案将会出现，以缓解不利因素：“从风险投资的角度来看，任何有助于你与同事和同事保持更紧密联系的事情都会很有趣，对吗？”
就“黑人生命也是命”(Black Lives Matter)运动引发的多样性问题而言，鲍尔德顿支持欧洲多元化风险投资等倡议。
“如果你有一个更多元化的风险投资行业，他们就会开始支持更多元化的创始人，做更多样化的事情，这自然会传播开来。这对我来说真的很重要，这也是我们关注…的一大部分。。
“在过去的三年里，我们在投资团队中雇佣的女性人数超过了男性人数。我们最近直接聘请了我们的第一位女性普通合伙人到…公司。合作伙伴中又多了三个有色人种等等。所以它开始改变到它应该在的位置，但我认为这是你必须在多条战线上战斗的其中一件事。“
note_en:
Last year Balderton raised a $400 million fund. But has the way that fund is being invested changed because of COVID-19?
 “In many ways, nothing has changed,” he said. ”We have been a series a focused pan-European VC for 20 years… If anything, I think COVID-19 has demonstrated how tech can help us get through various challenges, and I mean all of the work from home stuff…It’s been really weird, not being able to spend time in person with [entrepreneurs] those people… But the overall strategy of investing in tech in Europe, it’s exactly the same as it was before.”
  Although it’s not that simple. For instance, Balderton invested in car rental startup  Virtuo to the tune of 20 million euros. And travel is not exactly a great sector right now.
 Chandratillake admitted, “some industries we have had challenges this year.” However, he said they “had a difficult April and May, but they’ve actually had a booming August” as holidays came back.
 “I would say that by and large, most [startups] have navigated fairly well.” He noted that European governments have put in place funds to support tech companies, and of course, other sectors of tech have boomed.
 During the pandemic lockdown, many consumers jumped into virtual networking via apps like  Zoom   and Houseparty, but Balderton did a small investment into a stealth-mode startup called Riff, which, not unlike Clubhouse, is using audio in a new way. He hinted that this will be an enterprise-play on Audio.
 “Funnily enough, the closest to it right now is probably Discord which obviously is already a large network, but really a very much a vertical app aimed at gamers… But I think there’s a there’s an opportunity to do something similar in enterprise in the same way that Slack, you know, arguably got a lot of its initial cues from consumer messaging [such as] from WhatsApp or  Facebook   Messenger. I think we’ll see a similar thing where the enterprise gets something that’s based a bit on what we’ve seen in consumer products.”
 He said Riff solves the “classic cliche of the watercooler moment when you bump into someone in the office and have a chat, and it’s really hard to do that in this new reality.”
 He also said there are interesting sub-markets following on in the coattails of Zoom “that also need to be worked out.” Balderton invested in a company called DemoDesk (a cloud-based screen sharing platform), which looks at, for example, “webinars and sales meetings and specific kinds of conversations like that, where the requirements are a bit different.”
  Chandratillake is of the opinion that the world will have to live with COVID-19 for many years, but that new solutions will emerge to mitigate the downsides: “Anything that helps you stay more connected to your colleagues and your co-workers is going to be interesting from a VC point of view, right?”
 In terms of the diversity issues thrown up by the Black Lives Matter movement, Balderton has backed initiatives such as  Diversity VC in Europe.
 “If you’ve got a more diverse venture capital industry, they will start to back more diverse founders doing more diverse things, and that will naturally propagate. That’s really important to me and that’s a big part of what we focused on….
 “In the last three years, we’ve hired more women than we have men into the investment team. We recently hired our first female general partner directly into the firm… three more people of color in the partnership and so on. So it’s beginning to change to where it should be, but I think it’s one of these things where you have to battle on many fronts.”
title: Balderton的Chandatillake向俱乐部脱帽，称企业音频是下一个
title_en: Balderton’s Chandratillake doffs his cap to Clubhouse, says enterprise audio is next

note:
Shadow是一项面向游戏玩家的云计算服务。每月支付订阅费，您就可以在您附近的数据中心访问游戏PC。与GeForce now或Google的Stadia等其他云游戏服务相比，Shadow提供了完整的Windows10实例。您可以安装任何您想要的东西-Steam、Photoshop或Word。
在过去的几年里，该公司发展迅速，总共筹集了超过1亿美元的资金。去年，该公司宣布了雄心勃勃的计划，与OVHcloud和高端配置建立了广泛的合作伙伴关系。
与此同时，联合创始人伊曼纽尔·弗洛因德(Emmanuel Freund)辞去首席执行官一职，由杰罗姆·阿诺(Jérôme Arnaud)接任。新产品的推出已经多次推迟，该公司不再与OVHcloud合作。弗洛伊德于4月份离开了公司，正如Inpact Hardware在7月份报道的那样，阿诺已经离开公司几个月了。
所有这些都将我们引向今天的公告。该公司新任首席执行官迈克·费舍尔(Mike Fischer)在视频游戏行业相当活跃。过去，他曾在世嘉、万代南科、微软和Epic Games工作过。他在2010年至2013年期间担任Square Enix的总裁兼首席执行官。
Jean-Baptiste Kempf是开源社区的知名人物。在过去的14年里，他一直担任流行媒体播放器VLC背后的组织VideoLAN的总裁。VideoLan还对广泛使用的视频编码技术做出了贡献。他还创立了VideoLabs，这是一家致力于VLC相关集成和支持的公司。
该公司仍在努力向预购的欧洲用户推出新的Ultra和Infinition配置。该公司原计划从今年夏天开始在美国推出新的层级，但该公司现在表示，预计将在今年年底推出这些新的层级。
对于美国的客户来说，没有预购，当你的账户可用时，只会有一个升级按钮。LG在今年早些时候投资了该公司，这项服务也将于今年晚些时候在韩国上线。
note_en:
Shadow is a cloud computing service for gamers. For a monthly subscription fee, you can access a gaming PC in a data center near you. Compared to other cloud gaming services, such as GeForce Now or Google’s Stadia, Shadow provides a full Windows 10 instance. You can install anything you want — Steam, Photoshop or Word.
 The company has been growing rapidly over the past few years and raised more than $100 million in total. Last year, the company announced  ambitious plans with a wide-ranging partnership with  OVHcloud and high-end configurations.
  At the same time, co-founder Emmanuel Freund stepped aside as CEO with Jérôme Arnaud taking over. There have been multiple delays with the new product offering and the company is no longer working with OVHcloud. Freund left the company in April and, as INpact Hardware  reported in July, Arnaud has been on the way out for a couple of months.
 All of this leads us to today’s announcement. Mike Fischer, the company’s new CEO, has been quite active in the video game industry. In the past, he has worked at Sega, Bandai Namco, Microsoft and Epic Games. He was the president and CEO of Square Enix between 2010 and 2013.
 Jean-Baptiste Kempf is a well-known figure in the open source community. For the past 14 years, he has been the president of  VideoLAN, the organization behind popular media player VLC. VideoLAN has also contributed to widely used video encoding technologies. He also founded  VideoLabs, a company that works on VLC-related integrations and support.
 The company is still working on rolling out the new Ultra and Infinite configurations to European users who pre-ordered. It  originally planned to start rolling out new tiers in the U.S. starting this summer but the company now says it expects to launch these new tiers by the end of the year.
 For customers in the U.S., there are no pre-orders, there will simply be a button to upgrade in your account when it’s available. LG invested in the company earlier this year and the service will go live in South Korea later this year as well.
title: 云游戏平台Shadow获得新CEO兼CTO
title_en: Cloud gaming platform Shadow gets a new CEO and CTO

note:
B系列资金将用于满足增加的需求，缩短送货时间，并建立一套乘客服务解决方案。它还旨在提高其在北美、欧洲和日本的电动自行车市场份额。
部分原因是新冠肺炎疫情导致通勤者放弃公共交通，电动自行车热潮正在兴起。
据报道，各国政府目前正在投资自行车基础设施，电动自行车市场将在未来六年内超过460亿美元。
联合创始人Ties Carlier评论说：“电动自行车的采用是不可避免的全球变化，现在已经发生了很多年，但新冠肺炎对此进行了绝对的涡轮增压，以至于我们正在接近一个临界点，让城市变得更好。”
Vanmoof表示，在全球停产期间，该公司实现了220%的全球收入增长，2020年前四个月的自行车销量比前两年的总和还要多。
Norwest首席执行官斯图·坎贝尔(Stew Campbell)表示：“塔可、领带和Vanmoof团队不仅打造了无与伦比的品牌和畅销产品，而且正在重塑世界各地的城市移动性。”
鲍德顿公司负责人科林·汉纳：“随着新冠肺炎危机冲击全球供应链，范穆夫对设计和生产的独特控制是使公司能够灵活而有效地做出反应的一个关键优势。此外，Vanmoof直接面向消费者的做法使该公司能够与他们的乘客建立密切的关系，这种关系将在未来几年因新产品和服务而得到加强。“。
Vanmoof在今年4月推出了新的Vanmoof S3和X3。我在这里回顾了S3，并在这里签出了较早的X2版本。
note_en:
The Series B funding will be used to meet the increased demand, shorten delivery times and build a suite of rider service solutions. It also aims to boost its share of the e-bike market in North America, Europe and Japan.
 Partly driven by the switch of commuters away from public transport because of the COVID-19 pandemic, the e-bike craze is taking off.
  Governments are now investing in cycling infrastructure and the e-bike market is set to surpass $46 billion in the next six years, according to reports.
 Ties Carlier, co-founder VanMoof commented: “E-bike adoption was an inevitable global shift that was already taking place for many years now but COVID-19 put an absolute turbo on it to the point that we’re approaching a critical mass to transform cities for the better.”
 VanMoof says it realized a 220% global revenue growth during the worldwide lockdown and sold more bikes in the first four months of 2020 than the previous two years combined.
 Stew Campbell, Principal at Norwest said: “Taco, Ties and the VanMoof team have not only built an unparalleled brand and best-selling product, but they’re reshaping city mobility all over the world.”
 Colin Hanna,   Principal at Balderton: “As the COVID-19 crisis hit supply chains worldwide, VanMoof’s unique control over design and production was a key advantage that allowed the company to react nimbly and effectively. Moreover, VanMoof’s direct to consumer approach allows the company to build a close relationship to their riders, one that will be strengthened by new products and services in the years to come.”
 VanMoof launched the new VanMoof S3 and X3 in April of this year. I reviewed  the S3 here and checked out the earlier  X2 version here.
title: 在封锁导致电动自行车热潮后，Vanmoof筹集了4000万美元的B系列资金，用于全球扩张
title_en: After lockdowns lead to an e-bike boom, VanMoof raises $40M Series B to expand globally

note:
它刚刚启动了即将推出的电动空中出租车服务VoloCity的旅行预售-尽管没有日期说明这项商业服务将在何时(或在哪里)启动。但是，如果你支付1000个可用的发射前预订中的一个-它的品牌是“VoloFirst”*-你就可以期待未来长达15分钟的飞行，在服务商业化发布后12个月内，无论何时何地。
Volocopter的一位女发言人告诉我们：“服务将在2-3年内开始。”“目前还没有定义城市，因为目前还不清楚我们联系的众多商业启动城市中的哪一个会参加这场比赛。”
限量版兜风的价格是300欧元(约合355美元)，其中将包括一段你的旅行视频和一张“限量版、个性化证书”。
基于我们的公开试飞和监管成就记录，我们已经在短短几年内为电动飞行在城市普及铺平了道路。Volocopter首席执行官Florian Reuters在一份支持声明中表示：“随着预订的开始，我们现在邀请我们世界各地的支持者和创新者加入我们的行列，成为首批体验这种新的、令人兴奋的移动性形式的人之一，”Volocopter首席执行官弗洛里安·路透社在一份支持声明中表示。
Volocopter的首席商务官克里斯蒂安·鲍尔(Christian Bauer)在另一份报告中补充道：“虽然空中出租车的最终认证仍在等待中，但我们确实有一个详细的现实时间表，在未来2-3年内推出VoloCity的商业航班。”他进一步指出，VoloFirst的机票购买者将能够获得其进展和商业推出计划的最新情况。
早在2011年，这家德国初创公司就进行了有史以来第一次纯电动多翼飞机的载人飞行，并用其全尺寸飞机演示了无数次公共飞行-包括2019年10月在新加坡滨海湾进行的公开试飞，以及2017年在迪拜进行的世界上第一次自动eVTOL飞行。
今年早些时候，该公司的C系列融资达到9400万美元，使其融资总额达到约1.32亿美元。
它是众多争相推出商业服务的飞行出租车初创公司之一。其他公司包括EHang，Lilium和拥有Vahana VTOL的空客。
*Volocopter并不保证“VoloFirst”出行的购买者将是空中出租车服务的首批公众用户-相反，它表示这些早起的人将拥有“首批”吹嘘的权利
note_en:
It’s just kicked off presales for trips in its forthcoming electric air taxi service, VoloCity — albeit, there’s no date on when exactly (or where) the commercial service will fire up. But if you shell out for one of the 1,000 available pre-launch reservations — which it’s branding ‘VoloFirst’* — you’ll be able to look forward to a future flight of up to 15 minutes, within 12 months of the service’s commercial launch, whenever and wherever that will be.
 “Services will start in 2-3 years,” a Volocopter spokeswoman told us. “Cities are not defined yet, as it is not clear which of the many cities we are in contact with for commercial start will make the race.”
  The price for the limited edition joyride — which will include a video of your trip and a “limited edition, personalized certificate” — is €300 (~$355).
  “Based on our public test flights and regulatory achievement record, we have paved the way to make electric flight in cities common in just a few years. With the start of reservations, we now invite our supporters and innovators around the world to join us and be amongst the first to experience this new and exciting form of mobility,” said Volocopter CEO Florian Reuter in a supporting statement.
 “While the final certification for air taxis is still pending, we do have a detailed realistic timeline to launch commercial VoloCity flights in the next 2-3 years,” added Volocopter’s chief commercial officer, Christian Bauer, in another, further noting that VoloFirst ticket buyers will be able to get the latest updates on its progress and commercial launch plan.
  The German startup undertook the first-ever manned flight of a purely electric multicopter back in 2011, and has gone on to demo numerous public flights with its full-scale aircraft — including public test flights at Singapore’s Marina Bay in October 2019 and the world’s first autonomous eVTOL flight in Dubai in 2017.
 The company topped up its Series C funding round to $94M  earlier this year, bringing its total raised to circa $132M.
 It’s one of a number of flying taxi startups vying to get a commercial service off the ground. Others include  EHang,  Lilium and  Airbus with its Vahana VTOL.
 *Volocopter doesn’t guarantee ‘VoloFirst’ trip buyers will be the  first public users of the air taxi service — rather it says these early birds will have “among the first” bragging rights
title: Volocopter开始预售其第一次空中出租车飞行-等待时间为2-3年
title_en: Volocopter kicks off presales for its first air taxi flights — with a wait time of 2-3 years

note_en: After raising their IPO price ranges, both JFrog and Snowflake priced above their refreshed intervals last night. At their final IPO prices, the two debuts are aggressively valued, showing continued optimism amongst public investors that cloud shares are an attractive bet, even if their growth is financed through a history of steep losses, as in […]
title: JFrog和Snowflake积极的IPO定价表明对云股票的强劲需求
title_en: JFrog and Snowflake’s aggressive IPO pricing point to strong demand for cloud shares

note:
如果发生这种情况，这家游戏平台公司预计每股价值将在44美元至48美元之间，高于最初设定的每股34美元至42美元的IPO价格区间。
鉴于软件公司最近表现强劲，Unity提高IPO价格区间并不令人意外。就在昨晚，专注于开发人员的软件公司JFrog和专注于数据的云运营公司Snowflake都将其首次公开募股(IPO)定价高于上调的价格区间。
在今天的公开市场上，对成长型软件股票的需求很大。Unity拥有投资者通常在该行业内寻找的东西：收入增长超过40%，毛利率在70多岁到80多岁之间，占收入百分比和总美元的亏损都在下降。
以每股48美元的价格，Unity将出售12.亿美元的股票，估值约为126亿美元。考虑到最近一个季度的收入(1.843亿美元)和年化运行率(7.374亿美元)，Unity的估值约为收入的17.1倍。(您可以使用往绩收入指标而不是年化运行率统计数据来增大倍数，或者使用远期收入估计来降低倍数。)。
今天晚些时候，当雪花和JFrog开始上市时，我们会更好地感受到公开市场的火爆程度，但Unity的上调定价对这三家公司来说都是个好兆头。无论最终价格如何，Unity Investors都将表现良好。该公司上一次融资是在2019年年中，估值约为60亿美元。早些时候的股东在交易中会做得更好。
相扑逻辑预计也将于本周首次亮相。如果你愿意的话，这里有更多关于IPO的信息。
note_en:
If that happens, the gaming platform company expects to be worth  between $44 and $48 per share, up from its preceding $34 to $42 per-share IPO price range that it  initially set.
 Unity   raising its price range for its IPO is not a surprise, given that software companies have been on a strong run lately. Just last night developer-focused software concern JFrog and data-focused cloud operation Snowflake  each priced their public debuts above raised price intervals.
  There’s plenty of demand for growth-oriented software equities on today’s public markets. And Unity has what investors are generally looking for inside that sector: greater than 40% revenue growth, gross margins in the high-70s to low-80s, and falling losses in both percent-of-revenue and gross dollar terms.
 At $48 per share, Unity would sell $1.20 billion in stock, and be valued at around $12.6 billion. Given its most recent quarter’s revenue ($184.3 million) and annualized run-rate ($737.4 million), Unity is valued at around 17.1x revenues. (You can make that multiple larger by using a trailing revenue metric instead of an annualized run-rate statistic, or lower it by using a forward revenue estimate.)
 We’ll have a better feel for how hot the public markets are later today when  Snowflake   and JFrog start, but Unity’s upward pricing bodes well for all three firms. Unity investors are set to do well, regardless of its final price. The company last  raised $125 million in mid-2019 at a valuation of around $6.0 billion. Earlier shareholders will do even better in the transaction.
 Sumo Logic is also expected to debut this week. More on that IPO  here, if you are so inclined.
title: 联合提高JFrog，Snowflake目标首次公开募股估值后的IPO价格区间
title_en: Unity raises IPO price range after JFrog, Snowflake target steep debut valuations

note:
Atlassian Corportate Development负责人克里斯·赫克特(Chris Hecht)在今天的公告中写道：“随着越来越多的客户过渡到我们的云产品，我们致力于通过培育一个强大的云应用生态系统来支持他们的旅程，以增强他们的体验并满足所有使用案例。”我们为我们的Marketplace中已经提供的4200多个应用程序以及我们已经提供的与Slake、Zendesk和GitHub等流行工具的集成感到难以置信的自豪。但现在不是固步自封的时候。Atlassian风险投资公司将促进我们对我们的客户所需的最佳工具和集成的持续投资，以推动下一波创新并管理他们现在和未来的工作。“。
该基金正采取三管齐下的方式。它将投资于为公司云产品生产产品的早期初创公司。其中包括Jira、Confluence、Bitbucket和Trello。
但它也将投资于正在努力扩大业务规模的老牌公司。考虑到该基金的规模，该公司将与其他风险投资公司合作进行这些投资可能也就不足为奇了。赫克特以Atlassian在Zoom、Slake、Invision、Process.st和Split.io的现有投资为例。
除了这两个集团，该基金还将投资于Atlassian Partner Program的成员，这些成员“希望增强他们的云服务和/或创造支持未来工作的新产品”。
在这种背景下，值得注意的是，Atlassian最近也在其生态系统中收购了几家公司，包括Code Barrel(Jira自动化背后的公司)、Mindville和Halp。
note_en:
“As more and more customers transition to our cloud products, we are committed to supporting their journey by fostering a robust ecosystem of cloud-based apps that enhance their experience and satisfy all use cases,” Chris Hecht, Atlassian’s Head of Corportate Development, writes in today’s announcement. “We are incredibly proud of the 4,200+ apps already available in our Marketplace and the integrations we already offer with popular tools like Slack, Zendesk, and  GitHub  . But this is no time to rest on our laurels. Atlassian Ventures will facilitate our continued investment in the best-of-breed tools and integrations our customers need to fuel the next wave of innovation and manage their work, both now and into the future.”
  The fund is taking a three-pronged approach. It will invest in early-stage startups that build products for the company’s cloud products. These include Jira, Confluence,  Bitbucket   and  Trello  .
 But it will also invest in established companies that are working to scale their businesses. Given the size of the fund, it’s maybe no surprise that the firm will partner with other VCs to make these investments. Hecht cites Atlassian’s existing investments in Zoom, Slack, InVision, process.st and Split.io as examples for this.
 In addition to these two groups, the fund will also invest into members of the Atlassian Partner Program that are “ looking to augment their cloud services and/or create new products that support the future of work.”
 In this context, it’s worth noting that Atlassian has recently acquired a few companies in its ecosystem, too, including  Code Barrel (the company behind Automation for Jira),  Mindville and  Halp.
title: Atlassian推出5000万美元风险基金投资其生态系统
title_en: Atlassian launches a $50M venture fund to invest in its ecosystem

note:
这一出人意料的加速时间表让许多开发者争先恐后地为App Review准备他们的应用程序，并使开发者们对iOS14发布日的计划变得复杂起来。
一些公司，比如流行的播客播放器overcast，只是简单地通知用户，它计划中的iOS14功能还没有准备好。
其他人则不那么宽容，他们指出，苹果决定发布iOS14，而不在开发者社区循环，正如开发者史蒂夫·特劳顿-史密斯(Steve Troughton-Smith)所说，“在本来压力很大的一年里，开发者增加了很多不必要的压力。”
此外，苹果的决定影响了那些选择等待支持iOS14的开发者。
通常情况下，开发人员通常会利用iOS发布日，通过新闻稿、博客帖子和社交媒体来宣传他们的应用程序的新功能。来自应用程序评论网站的新闻报道甚至可能包括对最受欢迎的应用程序的显著更新进行汇总，或者突出那些以有趣的方式利用iOS新功能的应用程序。
今年，开发者社区不再担心追逐新闻和赞誉，因为他们现在必须提前为iOS14的更新做好准备。
我知道这听起来有多烦人，但我认为这是我在苹果发布会后感受到的最负面的一次。
我并没有强迫自己这么做，但我确实做了很多工作，准备推出iOS14的“第一天”版本，希望能得到一些清单之类的奖励
因为我的大多数客户还需要一段时间才能使用iOS14，所以我整个夏天都在优先处理漏洞修复和我家的流行病/学校后勤(我们还好，只是很忙)。
和你们一样，我今年也在尽我所能。很快会有更多。
直到现在，提交到App Store的工作才开始进行。现在是英国时间晚上10点；iOS14将于明天发布。App Review可以是一个小时到几天的任何时间。在充满压力的一年中，这对开发人员来说是一大堆不必要的压力，https://t.co/O1NrtipW4k。
想知道苹果通过降低每一次发布日和媒体评论的价格，可能会为第三方带来多少百万美元的潜在收入。
消费者也可能会受到这一意外发布的影响，因为一些应用程序制造商警告用户，他们的应用程序可能无法在新的操作系统上正常工作，直到他们更新了兼容性。任天堂(Nintendo)就是一个引人注目的例子，他在推特上表示，其“动物穿越：口袋夏令营”(Animal Crossing：Pocket Camp)游戏在更新后将无法运行，并要求用户在迁移到iOS14之前等待。
然而，iPhone用户很快就会将他们的软件更新到最新版本。在今年夏天的苹果开发者大会之前，苹果发布了新的iOS数据，显示其于2019年9月首次亮相的iOS 13操作系统，自那以来已经在过去4年内发布的所有iPhone中的91%和所有兼容iPhone中的81%上安装了iOS 13操作系统。
这意味着，在大多数iOS用户迁移到新版本之前，iOS开发者几乎没有时间更新他们的应用程序。
我认为我是聪明的，在周一提交了用13SDK构建的更新，比可能的14GM发布和提交的热潮早了一天。
我觉得事与愿违。14个SDK应用程序正在以创纪录的时间进行优先排序和审查。我打赌有13个应用程序排在队列的底部。
真不敢相信他们只是把iOS14甩在身后。作为一个开发者，这感觉太可怕了。
苹果公司刚刚宣布iOS14将于明天发布，这一消息毁了大家的一天。还没有开发人员做好准备。您需要将此更新推迟一段时间。
在这一最新的失态之前，苹果App Store连续几个月严厉拒绝，这甚至导致苹果和Basecamp之间就其现代电子邮件应用程序嘿发生了巨大的争执，该应用程序因应用内购买规则而被拒绝。苹果对潜在的应用内购买损失的关注也使其一度拒绝了WordPress应用，迫使该公司在被公开召唤后罕见地发表了道歉声明。
现在，苹果公司正与堡垒之夜的制造商Epic Games在法庭上争夺苹果公司委托Epice拍业务的权利，而除了苹果应用商店之外，苹果公司没有其他手段来解决苹果手机市场的问题。它认为，像Etic这样大的公司不需要依赖苹果提供的服务，比如分销和Apple Pay，但根据苹果的条款，它是被迫这样做的。
开发商也一直在注意苹果在提交给法庭的文件中对其App Store业务的描述，称这是开发商“从中获益”的东西--这一措辞的转变激怒了一些开发商。毕竟，人们购买iPhone的原因有很多，但它运行应用程序的能力是其中一个很高的原因。
开发人员也注意到，苹果试图撤走Etic的苹果开发者账户，包括其相关游戏开发平台Unity的账户，以及Etic通过“登录苹果”为用户提供支持的能力。这也是苹果试图撤走Etic的苹果开发者账户，包括其相关游戏开发平台Unity的账户，以及Etic通过“登录苹果”来支持用户的能力。苹果方面的这些强硬策略让开发人员清楚地看到，苹果已经准备好并愿意利用开发人员对苹果工具的依赖来惩罚任何越轨的开发人员。
还有一个事实是，苹果一直是针对其App Store业务的反垄断调查的焦点，这揭示了该公司是如何达成特殊交易的，尽管它声称App Store是“一个公平的竞争环境”。
最近，苹果更新了其App Store规则，以更好地阐明其关于佣金的条款，并为新的游戏流媒体服务加入App Store找到一条途径。但其结果是，它的规则现在变得如此复杂，有如此多的分割和例外，以至于一些开发人员可能会对什么是允许的感到困惑。
除了开发者日益高涨的不满情绪，苹果还在第二天向开发者社区发布了iOS 14，他们和其他人一样，正试图在冠状病毒大流行期间发挥作用--这场危机已经完全颠覆了人们的日常生活。许多开发人员现在都在远程工作和在家教育孩子。他们可能也会受到新冠肺炎的直接影响，可能是生病的家人。
-苹果的硬件发布日期决定了他们的软件发布日期，而不是软件质量或就绪情况。
-大多数苹果公司似乎对外部开发者面临的挑战和条件知之甚少。Https://t.co/uWGwlkYEvl。
苹果还没有向公众或开发者解释其决定出人意料发布的原因。
note_en:
The unexpected and accelerated timeline left many developers scrambling to ready their apps for App Review and has complicated developers’ plans for the iOS 14 launch day.
  Some, like popular podcast player,  Overcast, simply informed its users that its planned iOS 14 features won’t be ready.
 Others are less forgiving, noting that Apple’s decision to release iOS 14 without looping in the developer community has added, as developer  Steve Troughton-Smith put it, “a whole lot of unnecessary stress on developers in an otherwise stressful year.”
 In addition, Apple’s decision impacts those developers who choose to wait to support iOS 14.
 Typically, developers will often leverage an iOS launch day to promote their apps’ new features via press releases, blog posts, and social media. News coverage from app review sites may even include round-ups of notable updates to favorite apps, or highlight those apps that have taken advantage of new iOS features in interesting ways.
 This year, instead, the developer community can’t worry about chasing press and accolades, as they now have to get their app ready for the iOS 14 update ahead of schedule.
 I get how whiny this sounds, but I think this is the most negative I’ve felt after an Apple event.
 I don’t push myself that hard, but I did do a lot of work to prepare to hit the “day one” release for iOS 14 with hopes of a reward in the shape of making some lists or something
 Since it’ll be a while before most of my customers use iOS 14, I spent the summer prioritizing bug fixes and my family’s pandemic/school logistics (we’re OK, just busy).
 Like you all, I’m just doing what I can this year. More soon.
 Only now do submissions to the App Store work. It is 10PM UK time; iOS 14 launches tomorrow. App Review can be anywhere from an hour to several days. This is a whole lot of unnecessary stress on developers in an otherwise stressful year  https://t.co/O1NrtipW4k
 Wonder how many millions in potential revenue for third-parties Apple might have wiped off the table by undercutting every launch day and press review
 Consumers may also be impacted by the surprise release, as well, as some app makers are warning users their apps may not work properly on the new OS until they’re updated for compatibility. One high-profile example is Nintendo, who  tweeted that its Animal Crossing: Pocket Camp game won’t function following the update and is asking users to wait before moving to iOS 14.
 iPhone owners, however, are quick to update their software to the latest release. Ahead of Apple’s developer conference this summer, Apple  released new iOS figures that indicated its iOS 13 operating system, which debuted in September 2019, had since been installed on 91% of all iPhones released within the last 4 years, and on 81% of all compatible iPhones.
 That means there’s little time for iOS developers to update their apps before a majority of the iOS user base has moved to the new version.
 I thought I was being smart by submitting an update built with the 13 SDK on Monday, a day before the likely 14 GM release and rush of submissions.
 I think it backfired. 14 SDK apps are being prioritized and reviewed in record time. I bet 13 apps are at the bottom of the queue.
 Cannot believe they are just shadow dropping iOS 14. That feels so horrible as a developer.
  Apple just ruined everyone’s day with the news that iOS 14 is dropping tomorrow. No developer is ready. You’ll want to hold off on this update for a bit.
 This latest gaffe follows months of heavy-handed App Store rejections on Apple’s part,  which even lead to a huge blow-up between Apple and Basecamp over its modern email app, Hey, which was rejected over in-app purchase rules. Apple’s increased attention to potential in-app purchase losses also saw it rejecting the WordPress app at one point, forcing the company to  issue a rare apology after being called out publicly.
 Now, Apple is  battling in court with Fortnite maker Epic Games over Apple’s right to commission Epic’s business when there’s no other means of addressing the iPhone market outside of Apple’s App Store. A company as large as Epic doesn’t need to rely on the services Apple provides, like distribution and Apple Pay, it argues, but is forced to by Apple’s terms.
 Developers have also been taking note of how Apple describes its App Store business in its court filings, calling it something developers “reap the benefits from” — a turn of phrase that  rubbed some developers the wrong way. After all, people buy iPhone for a number of reasons, but its ability to run apps is high among them.
 Developers have watched, too, as Apple attempted to yank away Epic’s Apple Developer accounts, including those for its related game development platform, Unity, as well as Epic’s ability to  support its users through “Sign in with Apple.” These hardball tactics on Apple’s part made it apparent to developers that Apple is ready and willing to leverage developers’ dependence on Apple’s tools to punish any developers who step out of line.
 Then there’s the fact that Apple has been the focus of  antitrust investigations into its App Store business which revealed how the company cut special deals, despite its claims that the App Store is “an even playing field.”
 Recently,  Apple updated its App Store rules to better spell out its terms around commissions and to find a path for new game streaming services to join the App Store. But the result is  that its rules have now grown so complex, with so many carve-outs and exceptions, that some developers may be confused about what’s permitted.
 In addition to this growing swell of developer resentment, Apple sprung the next-day release of iOS 14 on a developer community who, like everyone else, is trying to function during the coronavirus pandemic — a crisis that has completely upended people’s day-to-day lives. Many developers are now working remotely and homeschooling children. They may be directly impacted by COVID-19, too, perhaps with sick family member.
 – Apple’s hardware release dates dictate their software release dates, not the software quality or readiness.
 – Most of Apple seems to have *far* too little of an idea of the challenges and conditions faced by outside developers.  https://t.co/uWGwlkYEvl
 Apple hasn’t explained to either the public or developers the reason behind its decision for the surprise launch.
title: 苹果出人意料地发布iOS 14烧毁了开发者的善意
title_en: Apple burns developer goodwill with surprise release of iOS 14
